Faith unites the weak believer to Christ just as it does the strong one, and purifies the heart of one just as truly as of another; and every sincere believer is by his faith justified in the sight of God. Faith produces godliness, and creates effects that no other grace in the soul can do. In Christ all fullness dwells, and pardon, peace, grace, knowledge, and new principles are given through the Holy Spirit. The promises to those who share in a Divine nature will cause us to inquire whether we are truly renewed in our minds; let us turn these promises into prayers for the transforming and purifying grace of the Holy Spirit. The believer must add knowledge to his virtue, increasing his understanding of the whole truth and will of God. We must add temperance to knowledge; moderation about worldly things; and add to temperance, patience, or cheerful submission to the will of God. Tribulation produces patience, whereby we bear all calamities and troubles with silence and submission. To patience we must add godliness: this includes the holy affections and dispositions found in the true worshipper of God; with tender affection to all fellow Christians, who are children of the same Father, servants of the same Master, members of the same family, travelers to the same country, heirs of the same inheritance. Therefore let Christians labor to attain assurance of their calling and of their election by believing and doing good works; and this careful effort is a firm argument of the grace and mercy of God, upholding them so that they will not utterly fail. Those who are diligent in the work of religion shall have a triumphant entrance into that everlasting kingdom where Christ reigns, and they shall reign with him forever; and it is in the practice of every good work that we are to expect entrance to heaven.

We must be established in the belief of the truth so that we may not be shaken by every teaching; and especially in the truth necessary for us to know in our day, what belongs to our peace, and what opposes it in our time. The body is merely a tabernacle or tent of the soul. It is a weak and unstable dwelling. The nearness of death makes the apostle diligent in the business of life. Nothing can give such composure in the prospect or in the hour of death as knowing that we have faithfully and simply followed the Lord Jesus and sought his glory. Those who fear the Lord speak of his loving-kindness. This is how to spread knowledge of the Lord; and by the written word, they are enabled to do this.

The gospel is not a weak thing, but comes with power. The law sets before us our wretched state through sin, but leaves us there. It reveals our disease, but does not make known the cure. It is seeing Jesus crucified in the gospel that heals the soul. Try to dissuade a greedy person from his greediness; one ounce of gold outweighs all reasons. Offer to restrain an angry man from anger by arguments; he has no patience to hear them. Try to hold back someone immoral; one smile is stronger with him than all reason. But come with the gospel, and urge them with the precious blood of Jesus Christ, shed to save their souls from hell and to satisfy for their sins, and this powerful pleading makes good men confess that their hearts burn within them, and bad men, even an Agrippa, say they are almost persuaded to be Christians. God is well pleased with Christ, and with us in him. This is the Messiah who was promised, through whom all who believe in him will be accepted and saved. The truth and reality of the gospel also were foretold by the prophets and writers of the Old Testament, who spoke and wrote under influence, and according to the direction of the Spirit of God. How firm and sure should our faith be, who have such a firm and sure word to rest upon! When the light of Scripture is shone into a blind mind and dark understanding by the Holy Spirit of God, it is like the dawn that advances and spreads through the whole soul until it makes perfect day. As Scripture is the revelation of the mind and will of God, every person should search it to understand its sense and meaning. The Christian knows that book to be the word of God, in which he tastes a sweetness, feels a power, and sees a glory truly divine. And the prophecies already fulfilled in the person and salvation of Christ, and in the great concerns of the church and the world, form an unanswerable proof of the truth of Christianity. The Holy Spirit inspired holy men to speak and write. He assisted and directed them in delivering what they had received from him, so that they clearly expressed what they made known. Therefore the Scriptures are to be accounted the words of the Holy Spirit, and all the plainness and simplicity, all the power and all the appropriateness of the words and expressions come from God. Mix faith with what you find in the Scriptures, and esteem and reverence the Bible as a book written by holy men, taught by the Holy Ghost.
